summary: Linked Jigglypuff/Purin `ftPr_Init.c` as a 100% matching 1.74 KB unit by splitting the large old `ftPr_Init` translation unit at `0x8013C9C8`. The init/callback portion became `Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_Init.c")`, while the remaining Purin special-move code was moved into new `ftPr_SpecialHi.c/.h` source and kept represented by new assembly `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialHi.s` for linking. Config, splits, object lists, and headers were adjusted accordingly. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_Init.s` and switched `ftPr_Init.c` from nonmatching to matching in `configure.py`.
Shrank the `ftPr_Init.c` split from `0x8013C2F8..0x801422E8` to `0x8013C2F8..0x8013C9C8`, leaving the init callbacks and small helper functions reported as 100% matching.
Created a new `melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialHi.c` split at `0x8013C9C8..0x801422E8` with separate `.sdata` and `.sdata2` ranges, and added a corresponding `ftPr_SpecialHi.h`.
Added `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialHi.s` and updated `obj_files.mk` so the linked objects are `src/.../ftPr_Init.c.o` plus `asm/.../ftPr_SpecialHi.s.o`.
Moved Purin special-move prototypes out of `ftPr_Init.h` into `ftPr_SpecialHi.h`, and included `ftPurin/ftPr_SpecialHi.h` from `ftdata.c`.
Added an include from `ftPr_Init.c` to `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` and removed now-unneeded includes from `ftPr_Init.c` after the special-move code was separated.

Changed one Marth SpecialN float literal from `1.0471975803375244140625f` to `60 * deg_to_rad`, likely to reuse the project angle constant idiom; the slice does not explain whether this was required for the Purin link.
smart_moves: Split the old oversized Purin init/specials unit at a clean address boundary, allowing the small init portion to link while preserving the remaining nonmatching special-move code under assembly.
Kept build metadata consistent across `splits.txt`, `configure.py`, and `obj_files.mk`, which is essential when changing which source file owns a text/sdata/sdata2 range.
Reduced `ftPr_Init.c` dependencies after moving special-move code away, making the matching translation unit smaller and less sensitive to unrelated include/order effects.
Created a dedicated `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` and updated `ftdata.c` include coverage instead of leaving many unrelated special-move declarations in `ftPr_Init.h`.
decomp_lessons: When a large character file contains an early matching init section plus later nonmatching specials, splitting the translation unit can unlock a linked matching object without solving the whole original file.

A link PR may need both source movement and assembly movement: remove the old assembly for the matching range, add a new assembly file for the still-nonmatching tail, and update object lists to match the split.
For split-file work, update `.text`, `.sdata`, and `.sdata2` ranges together; this PR split `ftPr_Init.c` data from the new `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` ranges rather than only changing `.text`.

Headers should follow ownership after a split: declarations for `ftPr_SpecialHi`, `ftPr_SpecialLw`, `ftPr_SpecialS`, and `ftPr_SpecialN` functions were removed from `ftPr_Init.h` and placed in the new special header.
Keep evidence-grounded caution around incidental cleanups: the Marth `deg_to_rad` change is visible in the diff but its relationship to linking `ftPr_Init` is not explained by comments.
naming_conventions: `ftPr_Init_*` names are retained for Purin init callbacks/helpers such as `ftPr_Init_OnLoad`, `ftPr_Init_OnDeath`, `ftPr_Init_OnItemPickup`, `ftPr_Init_LoadSpecialAttrs`, and `ftPr_Init_8013C94C`.

The new special header follows the character/action naming pattern `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` and contains `ftPr_SpecialHi_*`, `ftPr_SpecialAirHi_*`, plus related special Lw/S/N declarations that were formerly in `ftPr_Init.h`.
The new include guard is address-based: `GALE01_13C9C8`, matching the start of the new split.
Function declarations in headers retain address comments such as `/* 13C8C4 */` and `/* 14222C */`, consistent with existing decomp header style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Boundary tactic: isolate matching `ftPr_Init.c` as `0x8013C2F8..0x8013C9C8` and move the rest of the prior file to a new `ftPr_SpecialHi` split starting at `0x8013C9C8`.
Build tactic: mark `ftPr_Init.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)` while keeping `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` as `Object(NonMatching, ...)` and linking the new `ftPr_SpecialHi.s.o` assembly object.

Data-split tactic: assign old `ftPr_Init.c` `.sdata` from `0x804D3E80..0x804D3E94` and `.sdata2` from `0x804D9C08..0x804D9C10`, with the new special file taking `0x804D3E98..0x804D3EB0` and `0x804D9C10..0x804D9C98`.
Matching report evidence shows every listed `ftPr_Init.c` function scored 100%, including `ftPr_Init_8013C360`, `ftPr_Init_UnkMtxFunc0`, `ftPr_Init_OnItemPickup`, and `ftPr_Init_8013C94C`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that link `ftPr_SpecialHi.c` after this split.
Search for other character init PRs that split an init file from special-move code at a clean address boundary.
Search for `GALE01_13C9C8` or `ftPr_SpecialHi.h` to inspect later header cleanup and symbol renames.
Investigate whether the `60 * deg_to_rad` change in `ftMs_SpecialN.c` was needed for matching/data ordering or was incidental cleanup.
